new_cascade_issues.title: Thermal Gradient Exceeds Limit
new_cascade_issues.description: Marble has a higher thermal conductivity than typical materials used in spacecraft, which could increase the thermal gradient across the optical bench beyond the 15°C limit.

new_cascade_issues.title: Shock Test Required
new_cascade_issues.description: Marble is brittle and may not withstand the shock loads during launch, necessitating a new shock test to validate its structural integrity.

new_cascade_issues.title: Mass Increase
new_cascade_issues.description: Marble is denser than typical spacecraft materials, leading to an increase in the total mass of the payload. This could violate the 35 kg mass budget.

new_cascade_issues.title: Solar Panel Mounting Issues
new_cascade_issues.description: Marble may not provide adequate mounting points or structural support for the solar panels, potentially affecting their deployment and alignment.

new_cascade_issues.title: Stray Light Concerns
new_cascade_issues.description: Marble's reflective properties could introduce stray light into the star tracker, affecting its performance and accuracy.
